translation.Short positions in context: events, sell: Short positions in context: events, sellers, and price action.
translation.Track when CEOs, board members, and othe: Track when CEOs, board members, and other insiders buy or sell shares in Danish companies.
translation.See which stocks are most shorted, trend: See which stocks are most shorted, trending, and most viewed.
translation.Read our analyses that combine short pos: Read our analyses that combine short positions with company events, insider trades, and market context.
translation.In-depth analysis of short positions in: In-depth analysis of short positions in Danish stocks.
translation.Name: Name
translation.Select stocks to \"My list\" by clicking: Select stocks to "My list" by clicking the yellow star on the details page (top right corner).
translation.No announcements in the last month: No announcements in the last month
translation.No short sellers with positions equal to: No short sellers with positions equal to or greater than 0.50%
translation.Every 15 minutes, the data in the app is: Every 15 minutes, the data in the app is updated with data from the Danish FSA's website, where information about short positions is continuously published. The timestamps for updates of short positions are based on the Danish FSA's data, not on the time when we fetched the information.
translation.According to EU regulations, a natural o: According to EU regulations, a natural or legal person who has a short position representing 0.1% or more of the issued share capital must report this to the Danish FSA. The Danish FSA aggregates these short positions for each stock and publishes them on their website. These percentage figures are the ones you see in the app.
translation.It is important to understand that, as a: It is important to understand that, as a short position only needs to be reported when it constitutes 0.1% or more of a company's issued shares, the actual short position could potentially be greater than the indicated percentage. This is because short positions below 0.1% are not included in the Danish FSA's data.
translation.EU regulations requires that a short sel: EU regulations requires that a short seller's position is disclosed when it constitutes 0.5% or more of the issued share capital. Additionally, the short seller must report any change of 0.1% or more in the short net position.
translation.When a short seller reduces their short: When a short seller reduces their short position to below 0.5%, the short seller is removed from the list of "Largest sellers" in the app.
translation.Some investors choose to take a short po: Some investors choose to take a short position as a strategic move in the market. This involves borrowing shares they do not own and selling them with the hope of buying them back later at a lower price, resulting in a profit. This practice may be motivated by an expectation that the stock's value will decline in the future, but it can also serve as a form of risk management in an investment portfolio.
translation.You can get more details by clicking on: You can get more details by clicking on a row
translation.An elegant and simplified solution for a: An elegant and simplified solution for accessing information on short positions in Danish stocks.
translation.Download the app from the App Store for: Download the app for free from the App Store for iPhone, iPad, and Apple Watch, or access the web application.
translation.Build a watchlist of the Danish stocks y: Build a watchlist of the Danish stocks you care about.
translation.You can now see at which price levels sh: You can now see at which price levels short sellers have opened and closed their positions. Since the actual execution price is never publicly disclosed, all figures are estimates based on the previous trading day's typical price (average of high, low, and closing price).
translation.The Price flow tab breaks down all discl: The Price flow tab breaks down all disclosed short changes into 2%-wide price bands, so you can see at which price levels the shorts are estimated to have been opened and covered
translation.When closing prices are enabled on the c: When closing prices are enabled on the chart, the same distribution is shown as a bar profile on the left edge of the chart.
translation.Tap the Help button for a full explanati: Tap the Help button for a full explanation.
translation.Days to cover shows how many trading day: Days to cover shows how many trading days it would take all short sellers to exit their positions if they traded at the 30-day average daily volume. A higher number means shorts are more trapped: Covering quickly would move the market against them.
translation.All prices are estimates. The actual exe: All prices are estimates. The actual execution price is never publicly disclosed.
translation.The percentage at the top is the latest: The percentage at the top is the latest aggregate short interest in the stock: The sum of all disclosed short positions, expressed as a share of the company's issued capital. The arrow next to it shows the change since the previous reported value.
translation.The percentile tells you how the current: The percentile tells you how the current short interest ranks against this stock's full history. 90th means it has only been higher 10% of the time. The 7d figure shows how much the short percentage has moved over the last week.
translation.The blue area is the short position over: The blue area is the short position over time. The yellow dashed line marks the latest value so you can see at a glance whether shorts are higher or lower than today. Use the period buttons (1W, 1M, …, Max) to zoom in or out.
translation.Toggle the bar-chart icon to overlay the: Toggle the bar-chart icon to overlay the daily closing price (purple) and trading volume (grey). When closing prices are visible, a small bar profile appears on the left edge of the chart (see below).
translation.every reported snapshot of the aggregate: every reported snapshot of the aggregate short percentage, newest first.
translation.the named short sellers currently disclo: the named short sellers currently disclosing positions of 0.5% or more, with their sizes and the dates of their latest changes.
translation.shares opened (red) and covered (green): shares opened (red) and covered (green) bucketed by the price at which the trade most likely happened. The Net column shows the direction in each band. Bars on the left of the chart visualize the same data.
translation.For each day where the aggregate short p: For each day where the aggregate short percentage changes, the change is converted into a number of shares (delta % × total shares outstanding). Because Danish disclosures must be filed by 15:30 the trading day after the trade, the trade itself is attributed to the previous trading day's typical price (average of high, low, and closing price), which is a closer approximation of the average traded price than closing price alone. Each delta is then placed in a 2%-wide price band.
translation.Tap the yellow star in the top right to: Tap the yellow star in the top right to add this stock to your personal list. From the main page you can then filter to only the stocks you starred.
translation.price_flow_date_note: Dates show when each price level was last active
Not enough data to compute price flow.
translation.Explore short positions over time, table: Explore short positions over time, tables and charts included.
translation.See every short seller holding 0.50% or: See every short seller holding 0.50% or more in a given stock.
translation.Everything you need to track Danish shor: Everything you need to track Danish short positions
translation.If you have any questions, feedback, or: If you have any questions, feedback, or need assistance, please email us at:
translation.A new service (Short Sellers) where you: A new service (Short Sellers) where you can get insight into hedge funds with significant short positions in Danish stocks (over 0.5%). You can see which positions they currently hold and which they have held historically.
translation.Do you have any questions, feedback, or: Do you have any questions, feedback, or need assistance? We are always ready to help you. Please feel free to contact us via email, and we will do our best to respond to your inquiry as quickly as possible.
translation.Please send your inquiries to the follow: Please send your inquiries to the following email address:
translation.We use cookies to collect statistics on: We use cookies to collect statistics on the usage of the website
translation.If you click the 'Accept' button, you co: If you click the 'Accept' button, you consent to our use of cookies for statistical purposes. We use this data to improve the user experience for you and other visitors. This data CANNOT be linked to you.
translation.If you click the 'Decline' button, we wi: If you click the 'Decline' button, we will not set cookies for statistical purposes. However, we will use a cookie to remember your choice and to support the functionality of the website.
translation.You can change or withdraw your consent: You can change or withdraw your consent at any time by clicking the green "cookie" button in the bottom left corner.
translation.You can read more about our cookie polic: You can read more about our cookie policy in the privacy policy.
translation.We use functional cookies that are neces: We use functional cookies that are necessary to ensure our website functions properly. These cookies enable us to provide essential features, such as saving your settings and ensuring you can navigate our site without issues. Without these cookies, certain features and services on our website would not be available.
translation.We use statistical cookies to understand: We use statistical cookies to understand how our visitors use the website and to improve the user experience. We use Google Analytics to collect and analyze data on how you interact with our site. These cookies have a validity of up to 2 years. The data we collect is used solely for our own purposes and helps us improve the website for you and other visitors. This data cannot be linked to you. Please note that we only collect statistics if you have given your consent. You can withdraw your consent at any time by clicking on the green "cookie" button in the lower left corner of the screen.
translation.This privacy policy is about how we prot: This privacy policy is about how we protect your privacy and ensuring the security of your personal information. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, and share your information when you use our website or app.
translation.We have made every effort to store as fe: We have made every effort to store as few personal data as possible and only collect statistics for our own use if you consent to our cookie policy.
translation.Personal data is any form of information: Personal data is any form of information about an identified or identifiable physical person. In other words, it includes all types of information that can be directly or indirectly linked to an individual. This can, for example, be a first and last name, a home address, an email address, or an IP-address.
translation.3. We are the data controller - how do y: 3. We are the data controller - how do you contact us?
translation.Zirium is the data controller for the pr: Zirium is the data controller for the processing of the personal data that we have received about you. You will find our contact information below.
translation.We may collect the following types of in: We may collect the following types of information when you use our app:
translation.We collect information about how you use: We collect information about how you use our website and app, if you consent to our cookie policy.
translation.We may collect information about the dev: We may collect information about the device you use to access our app, including device type and operating system
translation.We collect and log your IP address, and: We collect and log your IP address, and in case of email communication, we will store your email address, which will be used exclusively for communication with you.
translation.We may use your information for the foll: We may use your information for the following purposes:
translation.To provide and maintain our app's functi: To provide and maintain our app's functionality.
translation.To communicate with you, respond to your: To communicate with you, respond to your requests, and provide customer support.
translation.To improve our app and analyze usage pat: To improve our app and analyze usage patterns.
translation.To comply with legal and regulatory requ: To comply with legal and regulatory requirements.
translation.We do not sell your personal information: We do not sell your personal information to third parties.
translation.We use Google's Gmail as our email servi: We use Google's Gmail as our email service provider.
translation.We use Google Analytics to collect stati: We use Google Analytics to collect statistics on usage data for our own purposes, if you have consented to our cookie policy. These cookies have a validity of up to 2 years.
translation.7. Where your personal information origi: 7. Where your personal information originates from
translation.We log your IP address when the website: We log your IP address when the website or app requests data from our service. Your email is only obtained if you communicate with us via email.
translation.We use Google Analytics to collect stati: We use Google Analytics to collect statistics on how you use our website. Google Analytics gathers information about your device, browser, and how you interact with our service. This information is used to improve user experience and for analytical purposes. Google Analytics uses cookies to collect this data.
translation.We retain personal information at most 2: We retain personal information at most 2 year after your most recent email contact or your last use of the website or app.
translation.You have the right to withdraw your cons: You have the right to withdraw your consent at any time. You can do this by contacting us using the contact information provided in point 3 above.
translation.If you choose to withdraw your consent,: If you choose to withdraw your consent, it does not affect the legality of our processing of your personal information based on your previously given consent, up to the time of withdrawal. Therefore, if you withdraw your consent, it will only take effect from that point onward.
translation.Under data protection regulations, you h: Under data protection regulations, you have several rights concerning our processing of information about you.
translation.If you wish to exercise your rights, ple: If you wish to exercise your rights, please contact us.
translation.Right to access information (right to ac: Right to access information (right to access)
translation.You have the right to access the informa: You have the right to access the information that we process about you, as well as additional information.
translation.You have the right to have incorrect inf: You have the right to have incorrect information about yourself corrected.
translation.In certain cases, you have the right to: In certain cases, you have the right to have information about you deleted before our regular general deletion occurs.

In certain cases, you have the right to restrict the processing of your personal information. If you have the right to restrict processing, we may only process the information, excluding storage, with your consent or for the establishment, exercise, or defense of legal claims, or to protect a person or significant societal interests.
In certain cases, you have the right to object to our lawful processing of your personal information. You can also object to the processing of your information for direct marketing purposes.

In certain cases, you have the right to receive your personal information in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format and have this personal information transferred from one data controller to another without hindrance.
translation.You can read more about your rights in t: You can read more about your rights in the Data Protection Agency's guidance on the rights of data subjects, which can be found at
translation.10. Complaint to the Data Protection Age: 10. Complaint to the Data Protection Agency
translation.You have the right to file a complaint w: You have the right to file a complaint with the Data Protection Agency if you are dissatisfied with the way we process your personal information. You can find the contact information for the Data Protection Agency at
translation.We cannot guarantee the accuracy of data: We cannot guarantee the accuracy of data within the Danish Short Watch app (the app). This data is collected from Danish FSA’s web portal which is a database for company announcements filed by companies which under section 25 of the Danish Capital Markets Act are obliged to file information with the Danish FSA.
translation.We are not responsible for how users uti: We are not responsible for how users utilize the app or the information and materials published therein.
translation.Users are solely responsible for downloa: Users are solely responsible for downloading or using information and other resources publicly available in the app, including those accessed through external links.
translation.We cannot be held liable for delays or e: We cannot be held liable for delays or errors in data.
translation.We are not responsible for loss on the u: We are not responsible for loss on the user or any indirect losses incurred.
translation.Below 0.5%, updates occur only once it e: Below 0.5%, updates occur only once it exceeds 0.5% again.
translation.The FSA's homepage for publishing short: The FSA's homepage for publishing short positions is down at the moment, making it impossible to retrieve the latest data.
translation.FSA website updated warning: (8/1-2026) The Danish FSA (Finanstilsynet) has reopened access to aggregated short positions, but their solution is unstable, and some positions are updated only to be rolled back shortly after.
translation.announcement_body: See exactly what company executives and board members are buying and selling in their own stock. Every MAR Article 19 filing from Danish listed companies, extracted and searchable.
translation.AI extracted data. Verify with source li: AI extracted data. Verify with source link if uncertain.
translation.insider_help_what_body: MAR Article 19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation requires company insiders (managers, board members, and their closely associated persons) to publicly disclose any trades they make in the company's securities within two business days. This page lists all Danish companies for which such transactions have been reported.
translation.insider_help_count_body: The number next to each company is the total number of reported transactions we have registered. The date shows when the most recent transaction was published.
translation.insider_help_search_body: Search by company name or stock symbol. Sort by Name, Symbol, or Last updated.
translation.insider_help_tap_body: Tap any row to open the company's transaction page. There you see a breakdown of each insider's activity, individual transactions with dates and prices, and the original source document for each filing.
translation.insider_help_insiders_body: The top table summarises each insider's total bought, granted, and sold shares, along with their net position. Tap a row to filter the transactions below to that person only. Tap again to clear the filter.
translation.insider_help_types_body: A green badge means a buy, red means a sell, and blue means a grant or allocation (e.g. warrants or share awards). The original term from the regulatory filing is always shown as-is inside the badge.
translation.insider_help_period_body: Use the period buttons (1W, 1M, 3M, 6M, YTD, Max) to narrow transactions to a specific time window. The filter applies to the transaction date, not the publication date.
translation.insider_help_sentiment_body: The bullish / bearish / neutral signal at the top of the page summarises net buy vs. sell activity over the last 90 days based on total value traded. Grants count as buys for this calculation.
translation.insider_help_warning_body: An amber triangle next to an announcement means the AI noted a caveat about the extracted data, for example an ambiguous number format or a missing field. Tap "View source" to verify against the original PDF filing.
translation.MAR Article 19 transactions reported by: MAR Article 19 transactions reported by company insiders to the Danish FSA.
translation.Click a company to see its transactions: Click a company to see its transactions
translation.Failed to fetch insider transaction deta: Failed to fetch insider transaction details.
translation.Click a person to filter transactions be: Click a person to filter transactions below
translation.No transactions match the selected filte: No transactions match the selected filters.
translation.This page shows every short position ann: This page shows every short position announcement filed by this seller, grouped by stock. Each entry comes directly from the Danish FSA's public register.
translation.This list shows every named short seller: This list shows every named short seller that has disclosed a position of 0.5% or more in a Danish stock. The Danish FSA only publishes names above this threshold, so smaller positions are not shown.
translation.Use the search field to find a specific: Use the search field to find a specific fund or institution by name. Use the sort menu to order the list by name or by the date of the most recent position change.
translation.Tap \"Current\" to show only sellers tha: Tap "Current" to show only sellers that hold at least one active position right now. Tap "All" to include sellers whose last known position has since been closed or fallen below the reporting threshold.
translation.Click any row to open the detail page fo: Click any row to open the detail page for that seller. There you can see every position they have ever disclosed, grouped by stock, along with the date and size of each announcement.
translation.Each row shows the publication date and: Each row shows the publication date and the disclosed short position as a percentage of the company's issued capital.
translation.A green \"Current\" badge means this is: A green "Current" badge means this is the most recently reported position for that stock and it is still above the 0.5% threshold. A red badge means this was the last known announcement and the position has since fallen below the reporting level or been closed entirely.
translation.Tap the ticker symbol link above each gr: Tap the ticker symbol link above each group to go to the full details page for that stock, where you can see the aggregate short interest chart, all other short sellers, and historic data.
translation.Send a quick message. I usually reply wi: Send a quick message. We usually reply within 24 hours.
Thanks! We'll get back to you soon.
We'll reply to your email when we can.
translation.You didn't leave an email, so I can't re: You didn't leave an email, so we can't reply directly. But we read every message.
translation.What would you like to share?: What would you like to share?
translation.Email (optional, if you want a reply): Email (optional, if you want a reply)
Something went wrong. Please try again.
translation.Too many requests. Please try again late: Too many requests. Please try again later.
translation.Message must be between 10 and 2000 char: Message must be between 10 and 2000 characters.
Please enter a valid email address.
translation.feedback_intro: Your click helps us understand which analyses readers want, and which topics we should prioritize going forward. It only takes a moment, and we truly appreciate it.
translation.Want to say more? Contact us: Want to say more? Contact us
